title: The Four Noble Truths
justification: This talk provides the most complete and balanced coverage of all Four Noble Truths in a single coherent presentation, moving systematically through dukkha (with its three types), tanha as cause, cessation, and the Eightfold Path. It has excellent literary quality with the powerful concluding quote from Meiji Kyo describing an Arahant's experience, providing both doctrinal clarity and inspirational depth. The structure is clean and comprehensive, making it ideal as a spine onto which complementary material about individual truths can be grafted.
summary: This talk systematically explains all Four Noble Truths—suffering in its three forms, desire as its cause, the possibility of cessation, and the path leading to liberation. It concludes with an inspiring first-person account from the Thai nun Meiji Kyo describing the liberated mind's relationship to arising phenomena.

relevance.note: Directly and comprehensively teaches the Four Noble Truths
